reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce Copado DevOps Engineer with zero embedded systems, automotive, or radar engineering experience. The JD demands C programming, MISRA guidelines, AUTOSAR, RTOS, and RADAR system knowledge for an automotive/embedded Senior SW Developer role in Chennai. Not a single must-have skill is present in this resume. The candidate operates exclusively in Salesforce CRM, CI/CD pipelines, and release management — a completely orthogonal technology stack. There is no basis to consider this profile even for a different level of the same role. Do not engage further; reallocate sourcing effort immediately.
strengths: COPADO DEVOPS MASTERY: Delivered 30% reduction in deployment time at Trimble Inc. through Copado SFP and Metadata Format CI/CD pipelines — a strong, quantified outcome in her actual domain.

ENTERPRISE RELEASE LEADERSHIP: 4+ years at Atos Syntel managing multi-environment Salesforce production releases with 24/7 support, hotfix coordination, and cross-functional stakeholder management for telecom enterprise clients.

CERTIFICATION DEPTH: Holds four Copado certifications (Fundamentals I and II in both Metadata and SFP formats) plus Salesforce Certified Administrator and Platform Developer I — unusually comprehensive credentialing for a release engineer profile.
gaps: ZERO EMBEDDED/AUTOMOTIVE DOMAIN EXPERIENCE: The role requires C programming, MISRA, AUTOSAR, and RTOS — this candidate has never worked in embedded systems, automotive software, or any safety-critical software domain. This is a fundamental domain mismatch, not a skill gap.

NO RADAR OR ADAS KNOWLEDGE: The JD explicitly requires RADAR system knowledge and lists ADAS as an added advantage. The candidate has no signal — not even adjacent — to signal processing, sensor fusion, or automotive perception systems.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in resume; no JD maximum specified, so no penalty applied.
Candidate is based in Puducherry but worked in Chennai — location proximity is close enough not to apply a penalty, though in-office Chennai is technically a mismatch.
Candidate has 4+ years of IT experience, meeting the minimum threshold of 4 years total experience required by the JD.
JD gender preference is 'Any', so no penalty applies.
No college exclusion or preference specified in the JD; no adjustment applied.

hard_filter_failures: Domain mismatch — candidate profile is entirely outside the required embedded/automotive software engineering domain
availability_reason: Current role at Trimble Inc. is listed as March 2025 to Present with no end date or notice period mentioned.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce Release Engineer / Salesforce DevOps Lead
rare_assets: Dual-format Copado certification stack (both SFP and Metadata Format, Fundamentals I and II) — rare to see all four Copado credentials held simultaneously.
Agentforce AI component deployment experience — early adopter signal on Salesforce's newest AI platform layer.
self_reported_unverified: 30% reduction in deployment time — claimed without specifying baseline deployment count, team size, or independent validation method.
Bronze Accolade Award and Spot Recognition Awards — internal awards with no third-party verification or context on award criteria scope.
interview_flags: Probe: Has the candidate ever worked on any embedded C or firmware project, even academically or as a side project — any adjacent signal to the JD domain?
Probe: Why is this candidate applying for an automotive embedded SW Developer role given her entire career is in Salesforce DevOps?
Probe: Clarify notice period — current role is active at Trimble Inc. with no exit indication.
Probe: The MBA in HR Management (completing December 2025) suggests a possible career pivot — what is her actual long-term trajectory?
Probe: Confirm the 30% deployment time reduction — what was the absolute baseline deployment duration and how was measurement standardised?
